children._na_bucket_removed_note: OSCAR II "n/a" sub-bucket removed as accounting artifact (£105.84B). This bucket is structurally a duplication of provider operating spend and does not reconcile with published DHSC totals. Same pattern as the 2023 "Non-patient-facing NHS expenditure" removal in fix_oscar_2023_nhs.js, but the bucket is labelled differently in OSCAR II 2022.
children._note: Reduced from £116.5B by £113.9B to avoid double-counting with top-level NHS Provider Sector. Residual represents capital expenditure (PDC, capital loans), HEE training income, and trusts excluded from the TAC sample.

_data_notes.text: OSCAR II "n/a" NHS Trusts sub-bucket (£105.84B) removed as accounting artifact, consistent with the 2023 fix. NHS Provider Sector (£113.87B) injected from TAC FY 2021/22 as a top-level sibling of DHSC, then DHSC > NHS Trusts netted against it to avoid double-counting (residual £2.58B is capital + PDC). Root value corrected from £1,257.58B to £1,151.75B. Note: DHSC admin + NHS Provider Sector totals £148.54B for 2022 vs £220.17B for 2023 and £214.24B for 2024 — a ~£65B gap that is not explained by the NHS pipeline fixes applied here. This may reflect additional OSCAR schema differences between the 2022 and 2023/24 editions, or items reclassified out of DHSC into other departments in earlier years. Treat 2022 DHSC admin as a lower bound pending further investigation.
